grants.title: SBRI Healthcare — themed competitions
grants.summary: Themed competitions for innovations addressing unmet NHS need — open to digital, AI and device innovations. Includes an Investment Readiness programme.
grants.means: The flagship NHS-aligned non-dilutive funder. As much a credibility marker as a cash injection — an SBRI award signals NHS validation to later investors.

grants.title: NIHR Invention for Innovation (i4i) — Product Development Awards
grants.source: National Institute for Health and Care Research
grants.value: SMEs eligible for up to 100% funding
grants.summary: Translational funding for medical devices, IVDs and digital health technologies bridging proof-of-concept to real-world clinical adoption. Two-stage application.
grants.means: Best fit for a product with early clinical evidence that needs the push to adoption-readiness. The two-stage process means an outline can be tested cheaply before committing to a full bid.

grants.title: NIHR Efficacy and Mechanism Evaluation (EME) Programme
grants.source: National Institute for Health and Care Research
grants.summary: Funds evaluation of efficacy and the mechanisms behind health interventions, including digital and technology-enabled interventions.
grants.means: Relevant where the gap is hard clinical evidence rather than product build. Outline applications close 5 August 2026.

grants.title: Innovate UK — Biomedical Catalyst
grants.summary: Supports SMEs developing innovative solutions to health and healthcare challenges, from small projects through to large collaborative R&D.
grants.means: The broadest UK non-dilutive R&D funder for healthtech. Pairs well with SBRI/NIHR — different stages of the same journey.

grants.title: EIC Accelerator
grants.summary: Grant plus equity for deep-tech SMEs and startups. UK organisations are eligible following association to Horizon Europe.
grants.means: Six fixed cut-offs a year — predictable enough to plan a submission around. Next cut-off 8 July 2026; further cut-offs 2 September and 4 November.

grants.title: Horizon Europe — Health Cluster (Cluster 1)
grants.summary: Collaborative health research and innovation funding. UK organisations can apply as part of a consortium of at least three countries, one an EU member state.
grants.means: Big money, but consortium-bound — best approached as a partner in a larger bid rather than a solo route. The 2026–27 work programme is published; specific calls have fixed deadlines.
